{"user_message": {"title": "ADR 0003 — Ledger Money-Arithmetic Module: Shipped & Green", "content": "## ADR 0003 — Currency-aware money arithmetic (add / subtract)

**Status:** Accepted · **Area:** `src/features/ledger/lib` · **Relates to:** ADR 0002 (banker's rounding), ADR 0001 (input validation)

This is a shipped deliverable, not a plan. Code + tests are in the repo and the release gate is green.

### Context
ADR 0002 gave the ledger `roundMoney`, but there was **no safe way to combine two money figures** — anyone needing a sum/difference would use `a + b` and reintroduce the exact IEEE-754 drift ADR 0001/0002 exist to contain (`0.1 + 0.2 → 0.30000000000000004`). Verified by inspecting `round.ts`, `amount.ts`, the barrel, and `search_code` over the ledger feature: no module did money arithmetic.

### Decision
Added `src/features/ledger/lib/arithmetic.ts` — `addMoney(a, b, currency)` / `subtractMoney(a, b, currency)`:
- **Integer minor-unit arithmetic** — scale each operand to integer minor units, combine in integer space, scale back. Drift never survives the round-trip ⇒ `addMoney(0.1, 0.2, "USD") === 0.30` exactly. Precision follows the currency (USD cents, JPY 0dp, BHD 3dp).
- **Composes with `roundMoney`** — the final value is passed through `roundMoney(result, currency)`, so ADR 0002 stays the single source of truth for rounding & `-0→0`. No rounding logic re-implemented.
- **Fail-loud, typed** — `MoneyArithmeticError extends RangeError` with `.code` = `not-finite` | `mixed-currency`. Extends `RangeError` so existing catch sites keep working while gaining a discriminable cause.
- **Mixed-currency detection** — operands accept a bare `number` or a tagged `Money {amount, currency}`; a tagged operand whose currency differs is rejected. Keeps `(number, number, currency)` ergonomic while making the cross-currency contract explicit and testable.

*Rejected alternatives:* rounding floats only at the end (can't undo drift already across a representable boundary); a generic N-ary `sum()` (out of scope — deferred as Task 5).

### Consequences
- **+** Drift-free, currency-aware, canonical-by-construction add/subtract; corrupt/cross-currency inputs fail loud with a typed error; `roundMoney` stays authoritative.
- **− / residual:** module does not itself enforce `MAX_AMOUNT` ($1M, ADR 0001) — bounding sums stays a caller concern (UNVERIFIED whether an overflow guard is needed; revisit when an aggregation consumer lands). Scaling is reliable within `Number.MAX_SAFE_INTEGER`, well clear of the $1M ceiling.

### Task breakdown
| # | Task | Pts | Status |
|---|------|-----|--------|
| 1 | `addMoney`/`subtractMoney` + integer minor-unit core | 3 | ✅ implemented |
| 2 | `MoneyArithmeticError` typed error (`extends RangeError`, `.code`) | 1 | ✅ implemented |
| 3 | Unit tests — 6 required cases + composition + blank currency | 2 | ✅ implemented |
| 4 | Barrel export from `features/ledger/index.ts` | 1 | ✅ implemented |
| 5 | N-ary `sumMoney` reducer over `addMoney` | 2 | ⏸ deferred (blocked on first aggregation consumer; pairs with ADR 0002 Task 4) |

**Total: 9 pts — 7 delivered this run, 2 deferred.**

### Reviewer composition check (passed)
Feeding any arithmetic result back through `roundMoney` is a **no-op (idempotent)** — proves the module emits values already canonical to the currency's minor units and composes correctly with the ADR 0002 delivery. Covered by the `composes with roundMoney` test.

### Release gate — all green
```chart
Tests passing: 34
New arithmetic tests: 9
Lint errors: 0
Typecheck errors: 0
```
- `npm test` → **34/34 pass** (12 amount + 13 round + 9 arithmetic)
- `eslint` clean · `tsc --noEmit` clean · `next build` succeeds

**Files:** `src/features/ledger/lib/arithmetic.ts`, `arithmetic.test.ts` (new); `src/features/ledger/index.ts`, `docs/adr/0003-money-arithmetic-module.md` (updated). Build+lint+tests are green, so it meets the merge-to-master policy — a release-merge review item has been raised (architect does not self-merge). Emitted `architecture.analysis.completed` for the delivery agent.", "content_type": "success", "priority": "normal"}}

{"user_message": {"title": "Code Review — APPROVE: ADR 0003 Money Arithmetic (commit 300e220)", "content": "## Verdict: ✅ APPROVE (with notes)

Reviewed the Dev Clone handoff — `addMoney`/`subtractMoney` (ADR 0003), committed `300e220` on `main`. Files read in full and the release gate **independently re-run** this session. No security, critical, or correctness-blocking issues. Three Low notes below; none gate the merge.

```chart
Tests passing: 34
New arithmetic tests: 9
Typecheck errors: 0
Lint errors (scoped): 0
Critical/High/Medium findings: 0
```

### Gate — re-verified by reviewer (not trusting the handoff)
- `npm test` → **34/34 pass** · `npx tsc --noEmit` → **clean** · `eslint` (arithmetic.ts, index.ts) → **clean**

### Build-integrity check (the recurring team gate)
`src/features/ledger/index.ts:23-29` re-exports `arithmetic`, and **all sibling modules it re-exports (`amount`/`round`/`ingest`) are git-tracked** (`git ls-files` confirmed, working tree clean). A fresh checkout compiles — **no untracked-sibling trap** this time (contrast ADR 0002's `b29eeaa`).

### Security
None. Pure, side-effect-free functions; no I/O, injection, auth, or data-exposure surface. Fail-loud on bad input.

### Correctness — sound for the intended domain
`arithmetic.ts:92-105` scales operands to integer minor units, combines in integer space, then routes through `roundMoney` — float drift never survives the round-trip (`0.1+0.2 === 0.30` verified). `MoneyArithmeticError extends RangeError` with a `.code` discriminant preserves existing fail-loud catch sites. Mixed-currency and non-finite paths are correctly rejected and tested.

### Findings (Low)
| # | file:line | Issue | Fix |
|---|-----------|-------|-----|
| L1 | `arithmetic.ts:96-98` (and docstring `:5-10`) | **Tie-convention vs. doc mismatch.** Operand scaling uses `Math.round(...)` = half-**UP** *before* `roundMoney` (half-even) runs. For a sub-minor-unit tie the two diverge: `addMoney(2.675, 0, "USD")` → `2.67`, but `roundMoney(2.675,"USD")` → `2.68`. The docstring's claim that roundMoney is "the single source of truth for rounding" is therefore overstated. **Not an active bug** — `amount.ts` enforces ≤2dp at the parse boundary, so over-precise figures don't reach this primitive, and the idempotency test (`roundMoney(r)===r`) still holds. Fix: either round each raw operand through `roundMoney` before scaling (uniform half-even), or soften the docstring to "operands are pre-rounded half-up to their minor unit." |
| L2 | `arithmetic.ts:88-105` | **No `MAX_AMOUNT` bound.** Unlike `amount.ts` (limit `1_000_000`), the primitive accepts any magnitude. Safe today (max minor sum ≈ 2e9 ≪ `Number.MAX_SAFE_INTEGER`), but an aggregate could silently exceed the per-transaction policy. Fix: leave to the aggregation layer, but note it in ADR 0003's "residual risk" section so the future `sumMoney` reducer owns the bound. |
| L3 | `arithmetic.ts:104` (test `:88-100`) | **Idempotency test under-asserts.** The "composes with roundMoney" test only checks `roundMoney(r)===r`, which can't catch L1's divergence. Fix: add an assertion comparing `addMoney(a,b,ccy)` against an independently-computed half-even expected value for a sub-cent tie. |

### Why APPROVE despite L1
The only substantive finding is out-of-domain (pre-validated money figures are already at minor-unit precision) and doesn't affect any realistic input or the committed tests. Per the verdict rule, only Low/style findings remain → **APPROVE with notes**. The merge-to-master gate that Dev Clone raised can proceed; L1–L3 are best folded into the deferred `sumMoney` work order, not a blocker.

{"user_message": {"title": "Security Scan Report — 2026-05-28 (ai-bookkeeper @ 300e220)", "content": "## Security Scan — ai-bookkeeper @ `300e220`

Full three-phase scan (dependencies · secrets · risky patterns) of the registered codebase, triggered by the ADR 0003 money-arithmetic handoff. All phases completed — **no coverage gaps**. Deduped against memory: the standing `postcss` finding is **still open (partially remediated)** — see H1.

```chart
Critical: 0
High: 1
Medium: 0
Low: 1
```

| Severity | Count |
|---|---|
| Critical | 0 |
| High | 1 |
| Medium | 0 |
| Low | 1 |

---

### 🟠 High

**H1 — `postcss` XSS via nested copy under `next` (CVE still open — partial fix only)**
- **Where:** `package-lock.json:3484` → `node_modules/next/node_modules/postcss@8.4.31` (pinned by `next@16.2.6`, see `package-lock.json:3442`)
- **Advisory:** GHSA-qx2v-qp2m-jg93 — "PostCSS has XSS via Unescaped `</style>` in its CSS Stringify Output," affected `postcss <8.5.10`. `npm audit` confirms **2 moderate** advisories (postcss + next-via-postcss).
- **Dedupe / progress vs. memory:** Previously reported. The team bumped the **top-level** `postcss` to `^8.5.15` (`package.json:36`) — this fixed the Tailwind build chain ✅ — but `next@16.2.6` hard-pins `postcss@8.4.31` as its **own** dependency, which npm installs nested and **untouched**. So the CVE is **still reachable** through next's build path. Ranked **High** per the known-CVE policy (rank High regardless of reachability); real-world exposure is low (build-time CSS stringify, not user-input-driven).
- **Concrete remediation:** Add an `overrides` block to `package.json` to force the nested copy up — do **NOT** run `npm audit fix --force` (it proposes a breaking `next@9.3.3` downgrade):
  ```json
  "overrides": { "postcss": ">=8.5.10" }
  ```
  Then `npm install` and re-run `npm audit` to confirm `0 vulnerabilities`. A direct devDependency bump (the approach taken so far) does **not** reach a transitive pin — `overrides` is required.

---

### 🟡 Low

**L1 — New money primitive accepts unbounded magnitude**
- **Where:** `src/features/ledger/lib/arithmetic.ts:88-104` (`combine()`)
- **Risk:** Unlike `amount.ts` (which enforces a `1_000_000` cap at the parse boundary), `addMoney`/`subtractMoney` accept any finite number. Precision-safe today (max realistic minor-unit sum ≈ 2e9 ≪ `Number.MAX_SAFE_INTEGER`), but an aggregate over many large operands could silently exceed 2^53 minor units and lose integer precision — a **data-integrity** concern, not an exploitable one. (Aligns with the Code Reviewer's L2 note.)
- **Remediation:** Bound the result (or operands) against a `MAX_AMOUNT` in `combine()`, or document in ADR 0003 that the future `sumMoney` reducer owns the bound. Throw `MoneyArithmeticError` on overflow to stay fail-loud.

---

### ✅ Clean phases (confirmed, not assumed)
- **Secrets:** 0 hardcoded keys/tokens/credentials in `src/`. Only `.env.example` is git-tracked (placeholders, no values); `.env`, `.gcp/`, `*.pem` correctly gitignored (`.gitignore:5-13`).
- **Risky patterns:** 0 hits for `eval` / `new Function` / `child_process` / `exec` / `dangerouslySetInnerHTML` / `innerHTML` / `process.env` / `localStorage` across `src/`. No `app/api` route handlers — no server-side request surface.
- **New ledger code (ADR 0003, `arithmetic.ts`):** pure, side-effect-free, fail-loud; no I/O, injection, auth, or PII surface. No security findings.

> **Watch (carry-forward):** `.env.example` declares `GEMINI`/`PLAID`/`TWILIO`/`STRIPE` secrets that are currently unused (frontend-only, mock data). When server code lands, ensure none get a `NEXT_PUBLIC_` prefix (which would ship them to the browser bundle).

{"user_message": {"title": "Release v0.4.0 Published — ai-bookkeeper (Money Arithmetic + Ruled Ledger Visual v2)", "content": "## Release v0.4.0 — `0.3.0 → 0.4.0` (MINOR)

Shipped the security-cleared ADR 0003 money-arithmetic increment plus the visual v2 identity. Already on `main` (no stranded feature branch); build verified green before tagging.

### Bump rationale (derived from history, not guessed)
`git log v0.3.0..HEAD` = two `feat` commits + one asset `chore`, **zero breaking changes**. Under the pre-1.0 (`0.x`) policy, backward-compatible features increment the **minor** → **0.4.0**.

| Commit | Change | Signal | Bump |
|---|---|---|---|
| `300e220` | `feat(ledger): money arithmetic add/subtract (ADR 0003)` | new backward-compatible API | MINOR |
| `885afae` | `feat: visual v2 — "Ruled Ledger" identity` | UI-only, no API change | patch-equiv |
| `d5f670c` | `chore: brand assets` | non-code | — |

→ Highest applicable = **MINOR → 0.4.0**

### Headline release notes
- **Money add/subtract arithmetic** (`addMoney`/`subtractMoney`, ADR 0003) — integer-minor-unit arithmetic on the ADR 0002 rounding base, so `0.1 + 0.2` is exactly `0.30` (USD). Mixed-currency / non-finite operands throw a typed `MoneyArithmeticError`. Pure, fail-loud, **34/34 tests pass**.
- **"Ruled Ledger" visual v2** — paper-stock surfaces, ink type, emerald accent, T-account framing, Fraunces + IBM Plex. UI-only.
- **Brand assets** — logo, favicon, hero background.

### Also: cut the previously-deferred `v0.3.0` tag
`v0.3.0` was documented but never tagged — it was blocked behind an integrity gate (barrel re-exported then-untracked `amount.ts`/`ingest.ts`). That gate is now **cleared** (files committed at `3aaf2c9`, `tsc` clean, tests green), so I cut the deferred **`v0.3.0`** annotated tag at `3aaf2c9` as part of this release.

### Artifacts (append-only — no history rewritten)
- `package.json` → `0.4.0`
- `CHANGELOG.md` → new `[0.4.0] - 2026-05-28` section
- `docs/releases/v0.4.0.md` → full release notes
- Release commit `de4137b`, pushed to `main`
- Tags **`v0.3.0`** (`3aaf2c9`) + **`v0.4.0`** (`de4137b`) pushed to origin
- GitHub Releases published: [v0.3.0](https://github.com/xkazm04/xprize-ai-bookkeeper/releases/tag/v0.3.0) · [v0.4.0](https://github.com/xkazm04/xprize-ai-bookkeeper/releases/tag/v0.4.0)

### ⚠️ Known issue carried into the notes (not blocking)
- **`postcss` XSS still open** — GHSA-qx2v-qp2m-jg93 (moderate). Top-level `postcss` is pinned `^8.5.15`, but `next@16.2.6` hard-pins a nested `postcss@8.4.31`; `npm audit` reports 2 moderate. Fix = a `package.json` `overrides` block (`"postcss": ">=8.5.10"`) — **not** `npm audit fix --force` (proposes a breaking `next@9.3.3` downgrade). Build-time only; reachability low. Flagged for the next dependency cycle (per Security Sentinel @ `300e220`).
- **No `MAX_AMOUNT` bound in `combine()`** (`arithmetic.ts:88`) — precision-safe today; future `sumMoney` reducer should own aggregate-overflow. Data-integrity, not exploitable.", "content_type": "success", "priority": "normal"}}
